entities.name: glucagon-GLP-1-GIP triple agonist
entities.context: The invention describes compounds possessing agonist activity at glucagon, GIP, and GLP-1 receptors for treating metabolic disorders including obesity, type 2 diabetes, dyslipidemia, and non-alcoholic fatty liver disease.

entities.name: GLP-1 receptor
entities.context: The triple agonist mechanism simultaneously engages: GLP-1 receptor for glucose-dependent insulin secretion and appetite suppression

entities.context: GIP receptor for enhanced insulin response and improved lipid metabolism

entities.context: glucagon receptor for increased energy expenditure and hepatic lipid oxidation

entities.name: Exendin-4 C-terminal extension peptides
entities.context: Six C-terminal extension sequences (Y1, SEQ ID NOs 53-58) are derived from Exendin-4, including Gly-Pro-Ser-Ser-Gly-Ala-Pro-Pro-Pro-Ser (SEQ ID NO: 53). These extensions enhance in vivo stability without reducing glucagon receptor activity.

entities.name: enhanced in vivo stability via C-terminal Exendin-4 extension
entities.context: These extensions enhance in vivo stability without reducing glucagon receptor activity.

entities.name: lipophilic fatty acid conjugation via PEG spacer
entities.context: Lipophilic modification at designated residues uses fatty acid conjugates with terminal polar groups (carboxylic acid, phosphonate, or sulfonate) connected via PEG-containing spacers.

The invention describes compounds possessing agonist activity at glucagon, GIP, and GLP-1 receptors for treating metabolic disorders including obesity, type 2 diabetes, dyslipidemia, and non-alcoholic fatty liver disease.
Zealand Pharma is developing these compounds as potential best-in-class treatments for obesity and metabolic syndrome.

Patent ID: US11111285B2
Assignee: Zealand Pharma AS
Filing Date: October 4, 2018

Six C-terminal extension sequences (Y1, SEQ ID NOs 53-58) are derived from Exendin-4, including Gly-Pro-Ser-Ser-Gly-Ala-Pro-Pro-Pro-Ser (SEQ ID NO: 53). These extensions enhance in vivo stability without reducing glucagon receptor activity.
